actionLibraryList.title: Seated 90/90 Stretch
actionLibraryList.context: Alternatively rotate your hips, keep your upper body upright, and maintain a 90-degree angle at your hips and knees.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Sit on the floor with your legs split. Bend one knee and bring it out to the side, creating a 90-degree angle at your hip and knee.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Alternatively roatate your hip, keep your upper body upright, always keep a 90-degree angle at your hip and knee.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Complete the full range of motion.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749718178177.jpg","context":"1.Rotate your hips alternately.\n2.Maintain a neutral spine position.\n3.Keep your hips and knees at a 90° angle throughout."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Keep a steady breath.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage your core.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Ensure a full range of motion.

actionLibraryList.title: Barbell Squat
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with your feet hip-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
2.Hold the barbell and place it across your upper traps, just below the neck.
3.Keeping your chest up, drive your hips back.
4.Allow your knees to move forward in the direction your toes are pointing.
5.Aim to achieve 90 degrees at both knees, with your eyes forward.
6.Driving through your heels, lift your body back to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Lock the pulleys to the footboard end with the barbell attachment.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Squat until your thighs are parallel to or slightly below the floor.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Keep your chin up, chest out, back straight, core tight, and your toes in line with your knees.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Keep your breath steady. Inhale as you descend, and exhale as you ascend.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Initiate the ascending movement by engaging your glutes.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"context":"1.Load the barbell onto your traps and shoulders.
2.Keep your back straight and your glutes engaged.
3.Slightly bend your knees.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750230418406.jpg"},{"context":"1.Lower into a squat with an upright back until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
2.Align your knees and toes, and avoid letting your knees cave in.
3.Slightly rotate your feet outward.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750230423096.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you rise, inhale as you lower.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engaging your glutes.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your chin up, chest open, back straight, core tight, and your toes in line with your knees.

actionLibraryList.title: Standing Dual-Handle Chest Fly
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Adjust the handles to chest height.
2.Stand in a split stance with core engaged for stability.
3.Grip a handle in each hand and slowly open your arms out to the sides.
4.Exhale while engaging your chest to bring your hands forward for 1–2 seconds, actively squeezing the chest.
5.Inhale and slowly return your hands along the same path to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Anchor the pulley at chest height with two handles attached.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Start by establishing a neutral spine while standing
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Pull your shoulders down, lift your chest, and engage your abdomen
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Keep your arms extended to your sides to fully stretch your chest, with your palms facing each other the entire time
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Keep breathing. Exhale as you press forward, and inhale as you slowly return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Engage your pecs to press the handles forward, directly in front of your chest.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Extend your upper arms backward.\n2.Keep your back straight.\n3.Maintain a split stance.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748922037620.jpg"},{"context":"1.Bring the handles together level with your upper chest or collarbone.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748922041908.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale while pressing forward, inhale while returning.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Focus on feeling the chest contract and stretch.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep a slight bend in your arms to avoid excessive stress on the elbows.

actionLibraryList.title: Standing Dual-Handle Row
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Adjust both handles to a height just below the shoulder blades.
2.Grip the handles with palms facing each other, arms naturally extended and parallel to the ground
3.Keep your core engaged and stable, with shoulder blades depressed.
4.Retract your shoulder blades, keep elbows close to your sides, and pull the handles toward your body.
5.Pause briefly at the top, then return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Anchor the pulleys at lower chest height with both handles attached.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Start with a staggered stance on the footboard and hold the handles with your palms facing each other.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Engage your core to stabilize yourself.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Avoid flaring your elbows, swaying your body, or shrugging your shoulders at the top.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Keep breathing. Exhale as you pull the handles towards your chest, inhale slowly as you return to the starting position
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: The lats are responsible for retracting and drawing your arms in to pull the handles towards your chest.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Grip the handles with palms facing each other.\n2.Keep your back straight.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749009982456.jpg"},{"context":"1.Bend your elbows, keep your upper arms close to your sides, and engage your back.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749009985527.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale while pulling, inhale while returning.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Focus the effort on the lats.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your upper arms close to your sides at all times, shoulders down, and avoid excessive forward or backward swaying.

actionLibraryList.title: Standing Single-Leg Extension
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand sideways, supporting yourself on one leg, with the working leg slightly lifted.
2.Hold the support handle for balance and keep your torso upright.
3.Engage the quadriceps to extend the working leg forward until nearly straight.
4.Slowly control the leg back to the starting position, maintaining quadriceps tension.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1751509480572.jpg","context":"1.Slightly lift the working leg.\n2.Hold the support handle to stabilize your body."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1751509485398.jpg","context":"1.Extend the lower leg forward until it's almost fully straight."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you extend, inhale as you return.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Focus on activating the quadriceps as you extend your leg.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid leaning back or swinging your thigh to prevent using momentum.

actionLibraryList.title: Bench Lying Leg Raise
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Lie back on a flat bench, hold the support with both hands, and extend your legs straight.
2.Inhale and flex your hips, engaging your hip flexors. Lift your legs to fully engage your lower abs, then extend back to the starting position.
3.Keep your core stable and maintain body balance.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Lie on the bench with a dual grip on the edge of the bench to maintain stability.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Hold a dual grip on the edge of the bench to maintain stability.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Keep your core stable and maintain body balance.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Inhale and return to flex your hips, engaging your hip flexors.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Lift your legs to fully engage your lower abs, then extend back to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Lie back on a flat bench and hold onto the support for stability.\n2.Keep your core stable and maintain body balance.\n3.Straighten both legs.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750056973315.jpg"},{"context":"1.Lower your legs with controlled abdominal engagement.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750056976121.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Inhale and return to flex your hips, engaging your hip flexors.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Lift your legs to fully engage your lower abs, then extend back to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your core stable and maintain body balance.

actionLibraryList.title: Standing Dual-Handle Biceps Curl
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, keeping your core engaged.
2.Hold a handle in each hand, arms naturally hanging at your sides.
3.Keep your upper arms stationary and curl your forearms upward, exhaling, until your biceps are fully contracted.
4.Pause briefly at the top, then slowly lower back to the starting position
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Adjust the handles to ground level.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Stand tall with a handle in each hand.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Keep your upper arms firmly tucked against your sides.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Don't use body momentum or sway your body back and forth.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Exhale on your way up and inhale on your way down.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Squeeze hard on your biceps to bring the forearm and biceps together. Hold a count of two at the top.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Relax the shoulder blades, keeping the upper arms close to the body.\n2.Keep the lower back straight, chest lifted, and engage the core.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750402596609.jpg"},{"context":"1.Keep your wrists neutral, with palms facing yourself.\n2.Bend your elbows, keeping your forearms close to your upper arms.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1752198689185.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Inhale as you lower, exhale as you lift.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage your biceps to lift your arms, achieving full contraction at the top of the movement.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid excessive forward or backward sway of the body.

actionLibraryList.title: Bent Over Tricep Rope Overhead Triceps Extension（L0）
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand upright, chest lifted, and engage your core. Raise your arms overhead, holding the rope, with your arms by your sides and elbows slightly bent.
2.Use your arms to extend forward, straightening them.
3.Slowly return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748416931128.jpg","context":"1.Hold the rope with both hands behind your head, keeping your arms at your sides.\n2.Slightly bend forward at the hips, maintaining a straight back.\n"},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748416934587.jpg","context":"1.Fully extend your elbows and lock them at the top."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you extend your arms forward, inhale as you slowly return to the starting position behind your neck.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage your triceps, extend your arms overhead, and hold at the peak contraction for 1 to 2 seconds.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your elbows close to your head.

actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Lie back on a flat bench and hold onto the support for stability.\n2.Keep your core stable and maintain body balance.\n3.Straighten both legs.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750056973315.jpg"},{"context":"1.Lower your legs with controlled abdominal engagement.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750056976121.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Inhale and return to flex your hips, engaging your hip flexors.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Lift your legs to fully engage your lower abs, then extend back to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your core stable and maintain body balance.

actionLibraryList.title: Standing Dual-Handle Biceps Curl
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, keeping your core engaged.
2.Hold a handle in each hand, arms naturally hanging at your sides.
3.Keep your upper arms stationary and curl your forearms upward, exhaling, until your biceps are fully contracted.
4.Pause briefly at the top, then slowly lower back to the starting position
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Adjust the handles to ground level.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Stand tall with a handle in each hand.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Keep your upper arms firmly tucked against your sides.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Don't use body momentum or sway your body back and forth.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Exhale on your way up and inhale on your way down.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Squeeze hard on your biceps to bring the forearm and biceps together. Hold a count of two at the top.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Relax the shoulder blades, keeping the upper arms close to the body.\n2.Keep the lower back straight, chest lifted, and engage the core.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750402596609.jpg"},{"context":"1.Keep your wrists neutral, with palms facing yourself.\n2.Bend your elbows, keeping your forearms close to your upper arms.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1752198689185.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Inhale as you lower, exhale as you lift.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage your biceps to lift your arms, achieving full contraction at the top of the movement.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid excessive forward or backward sway of the body.

actionLibraryList.title: Bent Over Tricep Rope Overhead Triceps Extension（L0）
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand upright, chest lifted, and engage your core. Raise your arms overhead, holding the rope, with your arms by your sides and elbows slightly bent.
2.Use your arms to extend forward, straightening them.
3.Slowly return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748416931128.jpg","context":"1.Hold the rope with both hands behind your head, keeping your arms at your sides.\n2.Slightly bend forward at the hips, maintaining a straight back.\n"},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748416934587.jpg","context":"1.Fully extend your elbows and lock them at the top."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you extend your arms forward, inhale as you slowly return to the starting position behind your neck.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage your triceps, extend your arms overhead, and hold at the peak contraction for 1 to 2 seconds.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your elbows close to your head.
